QF.id: XIII Trading, Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_58

question.text: Which of the following is a mandatory requirement for all clients trading in derivatives segments on Indian exchanges?

answer.text: Unique Client Code (UCC) registration with the exchange
Minimum net worth of Rs 10 lakh
Completion of a NISM certification exam
A minimum of 2 years of trading experience in equity cash markets

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Every client must have a Unique Client Code (UCC) registered with the exchange before placing any trade. The UCC ensures proper identification and tracking of all orders and positions at the client level, which is critical for regulatory surveillance and risk management. **The Trap:** Minimum net worth requirements apply to trading members and clearing members, not clients. NISM certification is required for intermediaries, not all clients. No minimum trading experience is mandated for clients. **Cross-Exam Context:** UCC is universal across all derivative segments — currency (Series I), interest rate (Series IV), equity (Series VIII), and commodity derivatives. Series XII (Securities Foundation) also covers KYC/UCC basics. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Ensure every client's UCC details (PAN, bank account, demat account) are up to date — mismatches can lead to trade rejection or regulatory penalties.
